was taken into custody for attempting to exit a conversation without saying "Right, I'll let you crack on then." Bail is set at £200,000.
was apprehended today after an elaborate heist plan was foiled by a small, yet strategically placed, 'Caution: Wet Floor' sign.
was intercepted at Tesco self-checkout after attempting to scan a bottle of gin as 'carrots'. Eyewitnesses report a heroic cashier saying, "Not today, mate."
was arrested at an M&S food hall after whispering "I could just live here" one too many times while browsing the Percy Pigs.
was taken into custody after attempting to merge into a queue without first asking "Is this the queue?" despite it being painfully obvious.
was caught red-handed attempting to leave a pub without doing the traditional "right, let's make a move" followed by another 20 minutes of standing near the door.
has been detained after repeatedly saying "goodbye" on a video call, only to continue talking for another five minutes. Authorities say they "had enough of this nonsense".
has been banned from all Greggs locations after attempting to pay for a sausage roll with 'exposure'. Witnesses say the cashier remained unimpressed.
was escorted out of Wetherspoons after committing the ultimate crime-ordering a full English without beans. Shockwaves ripple through the nation.
has been detained after attempting to cross a zebra crossing without the customary 'awkward jog' despite making direct eye contact with the driver.
was removed from a bus after not thanking the driver. Fellow passengers described the scene as "grim".
has been questioned after making a cup of tea using water that was merely "warm". Experts confirm this is not how it's done.
was apprehended after loudly stating "it's actually quite nice out" during light rain. Locals immediately grew suspicious.
was taken into custody after opening a crisp packet upside down. Authorities are calling it "needlessly chaotic behaviour".
was escorted out of a cinema after clapping at the end of the film. Witnesses say it was "a bit much".
has been detained after saying "let's circle back" in a completely normal conversation at the pub. Authorities are investigating.
has been questioned after putting the milk in before the tea. Experts have called for calm.
was taken into custody after describing a roast dinner as "just okay". Authorities are treating this as a sensitive matter.
has been detained after failing to say "sorry" when someone else bumped into them. Experts are baffled.
was escorted away after referring to the TV remote as "the clicker". Authorities are still investigating the origin of this behaviour.
was taken in for questioning after suspiciously not complaining about the weather for an entire day. Experts believe they may not be British after all.
was caught trying to leave a cup of tea unfinished. Authorities intervened before any further damage could be done.
has been sentenced to community service after failing to hold the door open for a stranger who was still 20 metres away. Locals are outraged.
was forcibly removed from a train carriage after saying "This seat's taken" without placing a passive-aggressive bag down as proof.
has been fined £100 for failing to do the mandatory 'little nod' when letting someone merge in traffic. Police call it an 'act of chaos'.
was caught trying to take the last biscuit from the tin without offering it around first. The King has been informed.
was arrested for reckless behaviour after responding to "alright?" with an actual detailed answer instead of just "yeah, you?". Shock and confusion ensue.
was caught in the act of leaving a voicemail instead of just texting. Psychologists are still trying to determine what led to this behaviour.
